title: Outputting Tables
text: vignette demonstrates output dataframes RTF using df_to_rtf function. df_to_rtf function takes dataframe input outputs RTF, format fairly opinionated, see.

title: Outputting Tables
text: First load package format example data. Next, ’ll use df_to_rtf function defaults, providing minimally required arguments. enforced opinions output: page header right-aligned page number page footer left-aligned snapshot right-aligned timestamp, well rows paths program output. also notice output: page header largely empty includes page number. table column names informative. page footer Output Name less desirable. Let’s improve issues generate informative RTF: Much better! Finally, things readily apparent. default, df_to_rtf function add following: stylesheet allows setting table title heading style level 1 4. table title set heading style level 2 default recognized Word. Bookmark start end tags around table. useful auto-linking within documents.
code: library(r2rtfExtras)  df       <- mtcars[1:10,] df$Model <- row.names(df) df       <- df[c("Model", "mpg", "cyl", "disp", "hp")] df_to_rtf(df              = df,           file_name       = "MyTable.rtf",           table_title     = "Table 1. MTCARS Dataset",           footer_snapshot = "Henderson and Velleman (1981)",            path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\tables.Rmd") df_to_rtf(df              = df,           file_name       = "MyTableImproved.rtf",           org             = "My Organization",           header_page     = "My MTCARS Analysis",           table_title     = "Table 1. MTCARS Dataset",           header_table1   = c("Model", "Miles Per Gallon", "Cylinders",                                "Displacement", "Horsepower"),            footer_snapshot = "Henderson and Velleman (1981)",            path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\tables.Rmd",           path_output     = "C:\\\\output\\\\path\\\\MyTableImproved.rtf")

title: Outputting Tables
text: can also output tables column spanning headers. Let’s see works: Currently, df_to_rtf allows one level column spanning, shown example output . key details: header_table0 specifies column spanning header row. header_table1 specifies column names second header row, row directly data rows. col_widths0 col_widths1 must sum value. alignment spanning row column names largely controlled col_widths0 col_widths1 arguments. col_just0 justification column spanning header row. col_just1 justification column names row data rows. happens inputs quite consistent? , col_widths0 col_widths1 sum value, header row consistent spanning row. can see , spanning header row, Performance, right border sligthly misaligned due inconsistency col_widths0 col_widths1 arguments. information inputs, check function documentation ?df_to_rtf.
code: df_to_rtf(df              = df,           file_name       = "MyColSpanTable.rtf",           org             = "My Organization",           header_page     = "My MTCARS Analysis",           table_title     = "Table 1. MTCARS Dataset",           header_table0   = c(" ", "Performance", "Engine"),           header_table1   = c("Model", "Miles Per Gallon", "Cylinders",                                "Displacement", "Horsepower"),            col_widths0     = c(1, 4, 4),           col_widths1     = c(1, 2, 2, 2, 2),           col_just0       = c("l", "c", "c"),           col_just1       = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c"),           footer_snapshot = "Henderson and Velleman (1981)",            path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\tables.Rmd",           path_output     = "C:\\\\output\\\\path\\\\MyColSpanTable.rtf") df_to_rtf(df              = df,           file_name       = "MyColSpanTableProblem.rtf",           org             = "My Organization",           header_page     = "My MTCARS Analysis",           table_title     = "Table 1. MTCARS Dataset",           header_table0   = c(" ", "Performance", "Engine"),           header_table1   = c("Model", "Miles Per Gallon", "Cylinders",                                "Displacement", "Horsepower"),            col_widths0     = c(1, 4, 4),           col_widths1     = c(1, 2.2, 1.7, 2.1, 2),           col_just0       = c("l", "c", "c"),           col_just1       = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c"),           footer_snapshot = "Henderson and Velleman (1981)",            path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\tables.Rmd",           path_output     = "C:\\\\output\\\\path\\\\MyColSpanTableProblem.rtf")

title: Extending 'r2rtf' for RTF Generation
text: goal r2rtfExtras provide additional helper functions assist creating RTF output r2rtf. primary wrapper functions df_to_rtf fig_to_rtf opinionated wrappers around boilerplate r2rtf code. additional capabilities currently yet built r2rtf. {r2rtf} package remains amazing tool generating output RTF format. Make sure read official documentation. package currently active development.

title: Extending 'r2rtf' for RTF Generation
text: package can currently installed Github repository using following (ensure devtools installed): See package website details.

what: Output a dataframe to a table in RTF with opinionated formatting. — df_to_rtf
title: Output a dataframe to a table in RTF with opinionated formatting. — df_to_rtf
text: df_to_rtf convenience wrapper around r2rtf functionality   outputting RTF table, optionally least one colspan column headers.

title: Output a dataframe to a table in RTF with opinionated formatting. — df_to_rtf
code: df_to_rtf(   df,   file_name,   org,   header_page,   table_title,   header_table0,   header_table1,   col_widths0,   col_widths1,   col_just0,   col_just1,   border_top1,   footnote_table,   cell_indents,   footer_snapshot,   path_program,   path_output,   font.family = 6,   col_header_bg_color = "gray94",   orientation = "landscape",   margin = c(1, 1, 0.75, 0.75, 0.5, 0.5),   title_as_heading = 2,   bookmark = TRUE,   bookmark_inc_title = FALSE )

title: Output a dataframe to a table in RTF with opinionated formatting. — df_to_rtf
text: df data.frame. Dataframe output RTF. Required. file_name character. File path RTF file saved. Required. Strongly suggested include spaces file name avoid issues bookmark functionality. org character. Organization name, placed top line page header, left-aligned line right-aligned page number. Default "" (organization name). header_page character. Header text page, typically name study. table_title character. Title table, table caption. Required. header_table0 character vector. Column spanning header table. Length vector must less number columns df. Optional. header_table1 character vector. Column headers table. Length vector must equal number columns df. Required. col_widths0 numeric vector. Relative widths column spanning header. Optional. used header_table0 entered. total sum col_widths0 must equal total sum col_widths1. col_widths1 numeric vector. Relative widths column headers. col_just0 character vector. Justification column spanning header. Optional. used header_table0 entered. col_just1 character vector. Justification column headers. border_top1 character vector. Border style top border column header. Optional. Length vector must equal number columns df. footnote_table character vector. Footnotes table, . cell_indents numeric vector. Indentation cell table, twips. Default 0, indentation. cell_indents must vector length nrow(df)*ncol(df). first value controls indentation cell row 1, column 1; second value controls indentation cell row 1, column 2; . footer_snapshot character. Data snapshot used, placed top row page footer. Required. path_program character. Path program generated table, e.g., "path//program.R", placed second row footer. Required. path_output character. Path output file, e.g., "path//output.rtf", placed third row footer. entered, use file_name. font.family numeric. Font family RTF document. Default 6 (Calibri). See r2rtf:::font_type() options. col_header_bg_color character. Background color column header rows, default "gray94". orientation character. Orientation page, either "portrait" "landscape". Default "landscape". margin numeric vector. value sets left, right, top, bottom, header footer margin order. Default c(1, 1, 0.75, 0.75, 0.5, 0.5). title_as_heading numeric. non-missing, table title inserted Word heading style. Expected input value 1, 2, 3, 4. Default 2. title style desired, set 0 specify function call. bookmark logical. TRUE, bookmark created RTF file name file (without extension). bookmark_inc_title logical. TRUE, bookmark placed RTF file title table included. Otherwise, title excluded. Default FALSE.

title: Output a dataframe to a table in RTF with opinionated formatting. — df_to_rtf
text: RTF file, file_name.

title: Output a dataframe to a table in RTF with opinionated formatting. — df_to_rtf
text: df_to_rtf creates formatted RTF file data frame input optional   colspans column headers. See example specify colspans.

title: Output a dataframe to a table in RTF with opinionated formatting. — df_to_rtf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df <- data.frame(Col1="1", Col2="2", Col3="3", Col4="4", Col5="5")  # Example: simple table without colspans df_to_rtf(df              = df,           file_name       = "MyTable.rtf",            table_title     = "Table 1. My table",           col_widths1     = c(3, 2, 2, 2, 2),           col_just1       = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c"),           footer_snapshot = "Analysis 2025",            path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\code.r",           path_output     = "C:\\\\output\\\\path\\\\MyTable.rtf")   # Example: table with colspans # - Cspan1 spans Col2 and Col3, Cspan2 spans Col4 and Col5 df_to_rtf(df              = df,           file_name       = "MyColSpanTable.rtf",            table_title     = "Table 1. My table",           header_table0   = c(" ", "Cspan1", "CSpan2"),            col_widths0     = c(3, 4, 4),           col_widths1     = c(3, 2, 2, 2, 2),           col_just0       = c("l", "c", "c"),            col_just1       = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c"),           footer_snapshot = "Analysis 2025",            path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\code.r",           path_output     = "C:\\\\output\\\\path\\\\MyColSpanTable.rtf") } # }

what: Output a figure to RTF with opinionated formatting. — fig_to_rtf
title: Output a figure to RTF with opinionated formatting. — fig_to_rtf
text: fig_to_rtf convenience wrapper around r2rtf functionality   outputting plot object RTF.

title: Output a figure to RTF with opinionated formatting. — fig_to_rtf
code: fig_to_rtf(   plot_obj,   file_name,   org,   header_page,   figure_title,   footer_snapshot,   path_program,   path_output,   font.family = 6,   fig_width = 5,   fig_height = 5,   orientation = "landscape",   margin = c(1, 1, 0.75, 0.75, 0.5, 0.5),   title_as_heading = 2,   bookmark = TRUE,   bookmark_inc_title = FALSE )

title: Output a figure to RTF with opinionated formatting. — fig_to_rtf
text: plot_obj plot object. object class `ggplot` `recordedplot`. file_name character. File path RTF file saved. Required. Strongly suggested include spaces file name avoid issues bookmark functionality. org character. Organization name, placed top line page header, left-aligned line right-aligned page number. Default "" (organization name). header_page character. Header text page, typically name study. figure_title character. Title figure, figure caption. Required. footer_snapshot character. Data snapshot used, placed top row page footer. Required. path_program character. Path program generated table, e.g., "path//program.R", placed second row footer. Required. path_output character. Path output file, e.g., "path//output.rtf", placed third row footer. entered, use file_name. font.family numeric. Font family RTF document. Default 6 (Calibri). See r2rtf:::font_type() options. fig_width numeric. Width figure inches. Default 5. fig_height numeric. Height figure inches. Default 5. orientation character. Orientation page, either "portrait" "landscape". Default "landscape". margin numeric vector. value sets left, right, top, bottom, header footer margin order. Default c(1, 1, 0.75, 0.75, 0.5, 0.5). title_as_heading numeric. non-missing, table title inserted Word heading style. Expected input value 1, 2, 3, 4. Default 2. title style desired, set 0 specify function call. bookmark logical. TRUE, bookmark created RTF file name file (without extension). bookmark_inc_title logical. TRUE, bookmark placed RTF file title table included. Otherwise, title excluded. Default FALSE.

title: Output a figure to RTF with opinionated formatting. — fig_to_rtf
text: RTF file, file_name.

title: Output a figure to RTF with opinionated formatting. — fig_to_rtf
text: fig_to_rtf creates formatted RTF file figure input.

title: Output a figure to RTF with opinionated formatting. — fig_to_rtf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create ggplot object plt <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x=mpg, y=disp)) +   geom_point() +   theme_classic()  fig_to_rtf(plot_obj        = plt,            file_name       = "MyFigure.rtf",             figure_title    = "Figure 1. My figure",            footer_snapshot = "Analysis 2025",             path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\code.r",            path_output     = "C:\\\\output\\\\path\\\\MyFigure.rtf",            fig_width       = 7,             fig_height      = 3.2)  # Using recordPlot with base R plot plot(1:10, 1:10, xlab="X-axis", ylab="Y-axis") plt_record <- recordPlot()  # Will return an error fig_to_rtf(plot_obj        = plt_record,            file_name       = "MyRecordedFigure.rtf",             figure_title    = "Figure 1. My figure",            footer_snapshot = "Analysis 2025",             path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\code.r",            path_output     = "C:\\\\output\\\\path\\\\MyRecordedFigure.rtf",            fig_width       = 7,             fig_height      = 3.2)  # Fix the class of the recorded plot object and try again class(plt_record) <- c(class(plt_record), "ggplot") fig_to_rtf(plot_obj        = plt_record,            file_name       = "MyRecordedFigure.rtf",             figure_title    = "Figure 1. My figure",            footer_snapshot = "Analysis 2025",             path_program    = "C:\\\\program\\\\path\\\\code.r",            path_output     = "C:\\\\output\\\\path\\\\MyRecordedFigure.rtf",            fig_width       = 7,             fig_height      = 3.2) } # }

title: Insert an RTF bookmark — rtf_bookmark
text: rtf_bookmark inserts RTF bookmark around RTF body contents.

title: Insert an RTF bookmark — rtf_bookmark
Insert an RTF bookmark — rtf_bookmark
text: rtf list. List 3 elements created r2rtf::rtf_encode. bookmark character. Name bookmark insert. unique name contain spaces. inc_title logical. TRUE, bookmark include table figure title. Otherwise, FALSE, title omitted. Default FALSE.

title: Insert an RTF bookmark — rtf_bookmark
text: input RTF object modified start end elements

title: Insert an RTF bookmark — rtf_bookmark
text: rtf_bookmark modifies RTF insert bookmark start   end tags within document. bookmark start tag inserted either   end RTF header (inc_title TRUE) immediately   table/figure content body (inc_title FALSE).   bookmark end tag inserted beginning RTF end element.

what: Transform RTF Title to Heading — rtf_insert_heading

text: rtf_insert_heading transforms RTF title specified heading style.

title: Transform RTF Title to Heading — rtf_insert_heading
Transform RTF Title to Heading — rtf_insert_heading
text: rtf list. List 3 elements created r2rtf::rtf_encode. heading_level integer. heading level apply title (1, 2, 3, 4). caption character. title already present RTF. font.family integer. font family number used RTF.

title: Transform RTF Title to Heading — rtf_insert_heading
text: input RTF object modified body

title: Transform RTF Title to Heading — rtf_insert_heading
text: rtf_insert_heading modifies RTF body change title   specified heading style. Note stylesheet defined   heading level must present title must bold.

what: Insert a right-aligned tab stop in the first line of an RTF page header or footer — rtf_tabstop
title: Insert a right-aligned tab stop in the first line of an RTF page header or footer — rtf_tabstop
text: rtf_tabstop inserts tabstop first line RTF page header footer.

title: Insert a right-aligned tab stop in the first line of an RTF page header or footer — rtf_tabstop
Insert a right-aligned tab stop in the first line of an RTF page header or footer — rtf_tabstop
text: rtf list. List 3 elements created r2rtf::rtf_encode. twips integer. position tab stop twips (1440 twips per inch). type character. Either "header" "footer", indicating whether modify page header footer.

title: Insert a right-aligned tab stop in the first line of an RTF page header or footer — rtf_tabstop
text: input RTF object modified body

title: Insert a right-aligned tab stop in the first line of an RTF page header or footer — rtf_tabstop
text: rtf_tabstop modifies RTF body insert right-aligned   tabstop. assumed first line header footer already contains   tab character, expected, .e., first line header   name organization followed tab character page number   field. first line footer data (snapshot) information followed   tab character date creation.
